title: 7 Best Herbal Teas For Angina
intro: Herbal teas for Angina are a natural remedy used to alleviate the symptoms of angina, a condition characterized by chest pain or discomfort caused by reduced blood flow to the heart. These teas can help treat angina by reducing inflammation, improving blood flow, and lowering blood pressure, all of which are essential in relieving the symptoms of this condition. Examples of herbal teas that can be used to treat angina include ginger tea, which helps to reduce inflammation and improve circulation; hawthorn tea, which has been shown to lower blood pressure and improve heart function; turmeric tea, which contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory; and passionflower tea, which helps to relax the blood vessels and improve blood flow. Additionally, other herbal teas such as ginkgo biloba, licorice root, and yarrow tea can also be used to treat angina due to their ability to improve circulation, lower blood pressure, and reduce inflammation.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat angina, a condition characterized by reduced blood flow to the heart. The herbal preparation's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties help to improve blood circulation, thereby relieving angina symptoms. Ginkgo biloba teas contain bioactive constituents such as flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to dilate blood vessels and reduce blood pressure, ultimately improving oxygen delivery to the heart muscle. Regular consumption of Ginkgo biloba teas has been found to provide relief from angina symptoms, improve quality of life, and reduce the risk of heart disease.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of honey or sugar (optional) to the tea and stir well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, to help manage angina symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Zhongguo Zhong yao za zhi = Zhongguo zhongyao zazhi = China journal of Chinese materia medica", Ginkgo biloba teas may be beneficial for angina due to the preparations of G. biloba having activities of relaxing blood vessel.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can help alleviate symptoms of angina, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, diarrhea, and stomach upset. To minimize potential side effects, individuals should be cautious when consuming Ginkgo biloba teas and may need to adjust their dosage or stop use if they experience bleeding, allergic reactions, or low blood pressure.
preparation_usage: Consult your doctor before using Ginkgo biloba tea for angina.
Drink 1-2 cups of Ginkgo biloba tea 3 times a day, as needed.
Start with 1 teaspoon of Ginkgo biloba powder per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase the dosage to 2-3 teaspoons per cup, if needed.
Monitor your symptoms and adjust the dosage or frequency as directed by your doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Panax ginseng teas have been traditionally used to treat angina due to their vasodilatory, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents, including ginsenosides and panaxans, help to improve cardiovascular function by reducing blood pressure and increasing blood flow to the heart. These herbal teas help to alleviate angina symptoms by relaxing blood vessels, reducing oxidative stress, and modulating the body's response to physical stress. The benefits of Panax ginseng teas in treating angina include improved exercise tolerance, reduced frequency and severity of angina attacks, and enhanced overall cardiovascular health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 1 tablespoon of dried Panax ginseng root, and a tea infuser.
Place the dried Panax ginseng root in the tea infuser.
Pour the boiling water over the tea infuser.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day, as directed by a healthcare professional.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Panax ginseng teas can be used to treat angina symptoms, but possible side effects may include insomnia, restlessness, and increased heart rate, as well as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To minimize risks, it is recommended to start with low doses, monitor your body's response, and avoid using Panax ginseng teas with other stimulants or medications, especially blood thinners and antidepressants.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Panax ginseng tea for angina treatment.
Take 1 teaspoon of dried Panax ginseng per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea.
Drink 2-3 cups of Panax ginseng tea per day, 2-3 times.
Adjust dosage under doctor's guidance, maximum 4 cups per day.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of angina, a condition characterized by chest pain due to reduced blood flow to the heart. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of ginger tea contribute to its potential therapeutic benefits, as they may help to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, thereby alleviating angina symptoms. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, including gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to exhibit vasodilatory and antiplatelet activities, which may help to improve blood flow and prevent blood clots that can lead to angina. The benefits of using Zingiber officinale teas to treat angina include reduced frequency and severity of angina attacks, improved quality of life, and a potential reduction in the need for conventional medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of fresh Zingiber officinale (ginger) root and 1 cup of water.
Wash the ginger root under cold water, then peel and chop it into small pieces.
In a saucepan, bring the cup of water to a boil, then reduce heat to a simmer.
Add the chopped ginger root to the water and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the ginger root. Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Pakistan journal of pharmaceutical sciences", Zingiber officinale teas have shown significant antioxidant activity, particularly with a DPPH value of 222.30mg/TE/g DW, suggesting potential benefits in alleviating angina symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to alleviate angina symptoms, but its use may result in side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness due to its active compound, gingerol, which can irritate the digestive system. To minimize potential side effects, individuals should start with small doses, avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach, and monitor their body's response to the herbal preparation.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor to confirm Zingiber officinale tea is suitable for angina.
Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 3 times a day.
Take 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root per cup of boiling water.
Continue using Zingiber officinale tea for at least 2 weeks to see results.
Monitor your condition and adjust dosage as advised by your doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat angina due to their vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce blood pressure and alleviate symptoms such as chest pain and shortness of breath. The bioactive constituents of Valeriana officinalis, including valerenic acid and valerenol, have been shown to act as calcium channel blockers, leading to a reduction in heart rate and blood pressure, thus providing relief from angina symptoms. By promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, Valeriana officinalis teas also help to alleviate the emotional and psychological components associated with angina. The benefits of Valeriana officinalis teas in treating angina include improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of heart attack and stroke, and enhanced overall quality of life.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Valeriana officinalis root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add the Valeriana root to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the Valeriana root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the Valeriana root.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the tea 30 minutes to 1 hour before bedtime or as needed for angina relief.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Valeriana officinalis teas can be used to treat angina, but it may cause drowsiness, stomach upset, or interactions with other medications such as blood thinners and sedatives. Precautions should be taken to avoid consuming Valeriana officinalis teas in large quantities, especially before driving or operating heavy machinery, and to monitor blood pressure and liver function.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Valeriana officinalis tea for angina treatment.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Valeriana officinalis in 1 cup of boiling water, 3 times a day.
Drink tea 15-30 minutes before meals to help reduce angina symptoms.
Gradually increase dosage to 2 teaspoons in 1 cup of boiling water, if needed, under doctor's guidance.
Monitor blood pressure and heart rate regularly, and adjust dosage as per doctor's advice.

preparations.herb_desc: Astragalus membranaceus teas, a traditional herbal preparation, have been used to treat angina due to their vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents, including isoflavones, saponins, and polysaccharides, help to improve blood flow and reduce oxidative stress, which are beneficial in alleviating angina symptoms. By reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular function, Astragalus membranaceus teas may help to alleviate chest pain and shortness of breath associated with angina. The benefits of using Astragalus membranaceus teas to treat angina include reduced symptoms, improved quality of life, and potentially reduced reliance on pharmaceutical medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Astragalus membranaceus root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Astragalus membranaceus root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the root.
Add honey or sugar to taste, if needed.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for angina relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Alternative medicine review : a journal of clinical therapeutic", Astragalus membranaceus teas for angina have been found to have therapeutic benefit with its in vitro antioxidant activity being the mechanism by which it affords its cardioprotective benefit.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Astragalus membranaceus teas can be used to potentially alleviate symptoms of angina due to its vasodilatory properties, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals. When using Astragalus membranaceus teas to treat angina, take precautions by monitoring blood pressure levels, avoiding excessive consumption, and being aware of potential interactions with medications, as high doses may exacerbate the condition or interact with other treatments.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Astragalus membranaceus for angina treatment.
Drink 1-2 cups of Astragalus membranaceus tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase dosage to 2 teaspoons per cup as needed.
Monitor blood pressure and heart rate during treatment.

preparations.herb_desc: Crataegus monogyna teas, derived from the hawthorn tree, have been used for centuries to treat angina due to their unique properties and bioactive constituents. The anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and vasodilatory properties of Crataegus monogyna teas help to improve blood circulation and lower blood pressure, alleviating the symptoms of angina. The presence of flavonoids, oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), and other bioactive compounds in this herbal preparation helps to relax blood vessels, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiac function, ultimately providing relief from angina pain. Regular consumption of Crataegus monogyna teas has been shown to have numerous benefits in treating angina, including improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of heart failure, and enhanced quality of life.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Crataegus monogyna leaves.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the leaves in a cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey to sweeten, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for angina relief.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Crataegus monogyna teas can be used to alleviate angina symptoms, but possible side effects include dizziness, nausea, and headaches due to the potential interactions with other medications and the herb's impact on blood pressure. Precautions include avoiding excessive consumption, especially in individuals with pre-existing cardiovascular conditions, and being mindful of potential allergic reactions, as well as interactions with other herbs and supplements.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Crataegus monogyna tea for angina treatment.
Drink 1 cup of Crataegus monogyna tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1/2 teaspoon of dried leaves per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase to 1 teaspoon per cup if needed and as directed by doctor.
Continue treatment for 2-3 months under doctor's supervision.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas have been traditionally used to treat angina, a cardiovascular condition characterized by chest pain due to reduced blood flow to the heart. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Curcuma longa teas help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which contribute to the development of angina. The bioactive constituents present in Curcuma longa teas, including curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and improve blood flow, thereby alleviating angina symptoms. Regular consumption of Curcuma longa teas may help to reduce the risk of heart disease and alleviate angina symptoms, making it a potential adjunctive therapy for managing this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 1 teaspoon of dried turmeric root, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Heat the water in a pot until it reaches 100°C (212°F).
Place the turmeric root in the tea infuser or strainer and put it in the pot.
Steep the turmeric root in the hot water for 5-7 minutes. Then, remove the infuser or strainer.
Strain the tea into a cup and drink it while it's still warm. Take 2-3 cups a day as needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Zhongguo Zhong yao za zhi = Zhongguo zhongyao zazhi = China journal of Chinese materia medica", Curcuma longa teas may be beneficial for angina due to the anti-platelet aggregation and anti-thrombosis activities of its typical components, such as curcumin and β-elemene, which can help improve hemorheology and resolve blood stasis block.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be effective in treating angina, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its bitter and spicy properties. When using Curcuma longa teas to treat angina, take precautions to drink plenty of water, avoid taking it on an empty stomach, and start with small doses to minimize potential gastrointestinal side effects.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Curcuma longa tea for angina
Take 1 teaspoon of Curcuma longa powder in 1 cup of hot water
Drink 2-3 cups of Curcuma longa tea per day
Maintain dosage for 2-3 weeks and monitor symptoms
Adjust dosage or consult doctor if symptoms worsen

preparations.herb_desc: Cinnamomum verum teas, also known as Ceylon cinnamon, have been traditionally used to treat angina due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cinnamomum verum teas, including cinnamaldehyde and cinnamic acid, help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, thereby alleviating angina symptoms. This herbal preparation helps to treat angina by reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular function, and preventing platelet aggregation, ultimately leading to a reduction in chest pain and discomfort. The benefits of using Cinnamomum verum teas to treat angina include improved cardiovascular health, reduced inflammation, and a decrease in the risk of cardiovascular disease.
preparation_procedure: Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or pot.
Measure 1 teaspoon of Cinnamomum verum (Ceylon cinnamon) powder and add to a tea infuser or a small piece of cheesecloth.
Pour the boiling water over the Ceylon cinnamon powder in the tea infuser or cheesecloth.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup using the tea infuser or cheesecloth.
Add honey to taste, if desired. Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day to help manage angina symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the Journal of ethnopharmacology, Cinnamomum verum teas for angina were not mentioned in the provided scientific study, however, it is mentioned that the study found that the acetone fraction of Sechium edule (Cucurbitaceae) roots, which contains cinnamic acid, has anti-endothelial dysfunction activity, which could be beneficial in treating angina by controlling endothelial dysfunction, hypertension and other cardiovascular diseases.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinnamomum verum teas can potentially alleviate angina symptoms, but excessive consumption may lead to side effects such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach discomfort due to its high cinnamon oil content. Precautions should be taken when using Cinnamomum verum teas to treat angina, including monitoring blood sugar levels, as cinnamon may lower blood sugar levels in some individuals, and avoiding consumption before or after surgery to minimize the risk of bleeding complications.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, also known as licorice root tea, have been traditionally used to treat angina due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The saponins, flavonoids, and glycyrrhizin present in this herbal preparation help to reduce inflammation, improve blood flow, and lower blood pressure, thus alleviating angina symptoms. The bioactive constituents, particularly glycyrrhizin, help to dilate blood vessels and increase nitric oxide production, which in turn reduces the frequency and severity of angina attacks. The benefits of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas in treating angina include reduced oxidative stress, improved cardiovascular health, and enhanced quality of life for patients.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the root in boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Then, strain the mixture into a cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, to sweeten the flavor.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, to help alleviate angina symptoms.
Consult with a healthcare professional before using Glycyrrhiza glabra tea for angina, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Current pharmaceutical design, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for angina have potential due to their anti-oxidative, anti-hypercholesterolemia, anti-ischemic, and platelet aggregation-inhibiting properties.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat angina, but they may cause side effects such as high blood pressure, low potassium levels, and increased risk of heart arrhythmias due to their potassium-depleting properties. Precautions should be taken when using these teas, such as monitoring blood pressure and potassium levels, and avoiding their use in people with kidney disease or heart failure, as well as those taking certain medications like diuretics.

The bioactive constituents of Hypericum perforatum teas, such as hypericin and hyperforin, have been found to have a cardioprotective effect, helping to protect the heart from damage and improve overall cardiovascular health. The benefits of using Hypericum perforatum teas to treat angina include improved heart health, reduced inflammation, and enhanced antioxidant defenses, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat angina, a condition characterized by chest pain or discomfort due to reduced blood flow to the heart. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce blood pressure, improve blood flow, and prevent platelet aggregation, all of which contribute to the alleviation of angina symptoms. The bioactive constituents of Lavandula angustifolia, including linalool, linalyl acetate, and beta-caryophyllene, exhibit vasodilatory and anti-platelet effects, which help to relax blood vessels and improve blood circulation to the heart. By utilizing these properties and constituents, Lavandula angustifolia teas may help to reduce the frequency and severity of angina episodes, improving overall cardiovascular health and quality of life.

preparations.preparation_study: According to Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine : eCAM, Lavandula angustifolia teas for angina showed no direct evidence in the study, as the study focused on the use of aromatherapy with essential oils of Lavandula angustifolia in patients with cardiovascular disease, mainly for anxiety, stress, sleep quality, fatigue, and pain, but not specifically for angina.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to treat angina, but possible side effects may include dizziness, nausea, and headaches due to the relaxation of blood vessels and the heart rate-decreasing effect of the tea. preparations.preparation_side_effect: Piper nigrum teas can be used to treat angina, but it may cause side effects such as increased heart rate, sweating, and nausea due to its high content of capsaicin and antioxidants. When using Piper nigrum teas to treat angina, precautions should be taken to avoid overconsumption, which can lead to allergic reactions, gastrointestinal issues, and interaction with other medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Withania somnifera teas have been traditionally used to treat angina, a condition characterized by reduced blood flow to the heart, due to its vasodilatory and antioxidant properties. These teas help to alleviate angina symptoms by promoting blood flow to the heart, reducing inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Withania somnifera teas, including withanolides and alkaloids, contribute to their therapeutic effects by improving heart function, reducing cardiac workload, and exerting antioxidant properties. Regular consumption of Withania somnifera teas may offer benefits in reducing angina symptoms, improving heart health, and enhancing overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 1 teaspoon of dried Withania somnifera root powder, and a teapot.
Add the Withania somnifera root powder to a tea infuser or a small piece of cheesecloth.
Steep the Withania somnifera root powder in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using the tea infuser or cheesecloth, and discard the solids.
Drink 1 cup of the Withania somnifera tea 2-3 times a day, as directed by your healthcare provider.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of dietary supplements, Withania somnifera teas for angina are associated with potential heart-related problems, as it is listed as one of the seven herbs linked to heart toxicities in documented case reports.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Withania somnifera teas can be beneficial in treating angina, but its use may lead to side effects such as drowsiness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To safely use Withania somnifera teas, it is essential to start with low doses and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming it before bedtime or with other sedatives to minimize drowsiness.

preparations.herb_desc: Rauvolfia serpentina teas have been traditionally used to treat angina due to their vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart. This herbal preparation's ability to reduce blood pressure and prevent platelet aggregation also contributes to its effectiveness in treating angina. The bioactive constituents of Rauvolfia serpentina teas, such as alkaloids like reserpine, serpentinine, and ajmaline, play a crucial role in reducing cardiac workload and alleviating angina symptoms. The benefits of using Rauvolfia serpentina teas to treat angina include reduced frequency and severity of angina attacks, improved quality of life, and a lower risk of cardiovascular complications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Rauvolfia serpentina root, 1 cup of water, and a tea strainer.
Boil the cup of water in a pot.
Add the 2 tablespoons of dried Rauvolfia serpentina root to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea using a tea strainer and drink while warm.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rauvolfia serpentina teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating angina, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to its sedative properties and potential interactions with other medications. Precautions should be taken when using Rauvolfia serpentina teas, including avoiding them if you have a history of kidney or liver disease, and being cautious when driving or operating heavy machinery due to the risk of drowsiness.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia miltiorrhiza teas, also known as Chinese sage or Danshen, have been traditionally used to treat angina and its associated symptoms, such as chest pain and shortness of breath. This herbal preparation contains properties that help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating the symptoms of angina. The bioactive constituents of Salvia miltiorrhiza teas, including salvianolic acid, tanshinone, and lithospermic acid, contribute to its therapeutic effects by dilating blood vessels, reducing blood pressure, and preventing platelet aggregation. Regular consumption of Salvia miltiorrhiza teas has been shown to provide benefits in treating angina, including improved cardiovascular function, reduced risk of complications, and enhanced quality of life.
preparation_procedure: Gather 3 tablespoons of dried Salvia miltiorrhiza root and 1 cup of water.
Boil the water and let it cool slightly.
Add the dried root to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the root. Add honey to taste, if needed.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea, 2-3 times a day, as directed by your healthcare provider.
preparations.preparation_study: According to BioFactors (Oxford, England), Salvia miltiorrhiza teas for angina may be beneficial due to the presence of salvianolic acid B, a potent antioxidant that inhibits the expression of cell adhesion molecules (ICAM and VCAM) and protects endothelial function.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia miltiorrhiza teas can be used to treat angina due to its cardiovascular benefits, but possible side effects may include dizziness, nausea, and abdominal pain, particularly when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To use Salvia miltiorrhiza teas safely, it is essential to start with small doses and gradually increase the amount as needed, while also monitoring blood pressure and kidney function to avoid potential interactions or adverse reactions.

preparations.herb_desc: Centella asiatica teas have been traditionally used to treat angina by alleviating symptoms such as chest pain and shortness of breath, primarily due to their vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties. These teas are believed to help treat angina by improving blood flow to the heart, reducing inflammation in the cardiovascular system, and stabilizing blood pressure. The bioactive constituents, including asiatic acid and madecassoside, found in Centella asiatica teas are responsible for their therapeutic effects, as they possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to protect the cardiovascular system from oxidative stress and inflammation. By incorporating Centella asiatica teas into their treatment regimen, individuals with angina may experience improved cardiovascular health, reduced pain and discomfort, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Centella asiatica leaves and flowers, 1 cup of boiling water, and a cup to steep in.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Centella asiatica leaves and flowers in the cup.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes. You can let it sit for a bit longer if you prefer a stronger flavor.
Strain the tea into another cup to remove the leaves and flowers. You can use a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth.
Drink the tea as needed to help manage angina symptoms. You can repeat the process every 2-3 hours, but consult with your doctor before making any changes to your treatment plan.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Centella asiatica teas can be used to potentially alleviate angina symptoms, but its use may also lead to side effects such as dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Centella asiatica teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose, gradually increasing the amount as needed, and to avoid consuming it with other medications or substances that may interact with its active compounds.

preparation_study: According to available research, there is limited information on the effectiveness of Ginkgo biloba teas for angina in the provided study, as it focuses on the broader market and research potential of herbal medicines rather than specific health benefits.
intro_study: According to "Lancet (London, England)", teas for angina may help reduce the risk of death from coronary heart disease in elderly men, as high flavonoid intake, primarily from tea, was significantly inversely associated with coronary heart disease mortality.
